# Product Analytics
## Purpose
Establish a robust product analytics practice with PostHog for events, funnels, flags, experiments, and session insights.

## Inputs Required
- Product goals and key metrics.
- Event taxonomy and naming conventions.
- User identity model and privacy constraints.
- Reporting and experimentation requirements.

## Step-by-Step Workflow
1. Define event and property taxonomy aligned to business metrics.
2. Instrument events at critical user journey points.
3. Configure feature flags and experimentation boundaries.
4. Enable session replay and funnel views for diagnosis.
5. Validate event quality, duplication, and attribution.
6. Document dashboards, owners, and review cadence.

## Validation Checks
- Events are complete, consistent, and non-duplicative.
- Key product metrics are computable from instrumented data.
- Feature flag and experiment events are attributable to cohorts.
- Privacy-sensitive fields are redacted or excluded.

## Output Format
- Instrumentation matrix (event, trigger, properties, owner).
- Dashboard and experiment mapping.
- Data quality and governance checklist.

## Integration Hooks (which agents use this)
- `lead-analytics-architect`
- `analytics-engineer`

## Anti-Patterns
- Event sprawl without naming governance.
- Tracking sensitive data without explicit policy.
- Running experiments without stable exposure/metric events.

## External References (official docs URL from tech stack)
- https://posthog.com/docs
